T001.did: Confirmed the baseline compiled and all 74 suites / 866 tests passed before any change.

T002.did: Replaced the tri-state swap decision with an add-only decideEnsureStandardOps that never removes companion-standard.
concerns: Made the steady state (companion-standard already present, no leftovers) a true no-op to satisfy the idempotency contract (FR-006 / Contract C / T008 / T014); the loose "enable when already installed" from T002 is emitted only when a leftover lean/legacy removal touched the shared command files.

T003.did: Extracted the add-only ensureStandardFamily runner from reconcileCompanionPreset, dropping remove-before-add ordering and the removeFailed skip.
concerns: ensureStandardFamily no longer mirrors the setting to companion.yml (the old reconcile did); that mirroring moved into extension.ts so seeding still reads the current default.

T004.did: Removed both destructive reconcile call sites — activation now mirrors the setting and runs the add-only ensure; the config-change handler only mirrors the setting.

T005.did: Added a test asserting decideEnsureStandardOps emits no remove of companion-standard across every installed-state permutation.

T006.did: Extended profileDispatch tests to cover all four lean twins, standard/absent/invalid pass-through, corrupt-context safety, and seedProfileForNewSpec with in-flight pinning.

T007.did: Deleted the dead swap machinery (PRESET_BY_PROFILE, tri-state target selection, off-removes-both, removeFailed guard); kept the installed-state probe.

T008.did: Rewrote the reconciler test for idempotency (already-present yields zero ops) and no standard-family remove on any mode change.

T009.did: Retired the right-click Template Profile submenu, its two setProfile commands, its menu items, and the context-menu reference in package.json.

T010.did: Removed the per-spec profile menu handler and dropped the now-unused setProfile import from specCommands.ts.
concerns: setProfile in stepLifecycle.ts is now unused but retained as internal plumbing per the plan (Decision 5).

T011.did: Seeded each new spec’s pinned profile from the project default at the specify-step skeletons via a shared seedProfileForNewSpec helper.
concerns: Seeded a third skeleton (stepLifecycle.buildFallback) beyond the two named in the task: startStep races updateStepProgress on the first write and uses buildFallback, so seeding only the two named sites would lose the pin when startStep wins.

T012.did: Rewrote the templateProfile setting description and enum descriptions to the routing model — no reconcile/swap, no retired menu.

T013.did: Verified ensureStandardFamily issues the bundled-path --dev add when companion-standard is absent and a no-op when present; presetCommandFor still emits the --dev form (covered by tests).

T014.did: Added a recovery test: a stranded state emits the bundled-path add; already-present emits zero ops.

T015.did: Rewrote the README Template Profiles section to the single-setting, non-destructive routing model.

T016.did: Rewrote the spec-kit extension README Template profiles section user-first: what each mode produces, how to switch via the setting, and the non-destructive guarantee.

T017.did: Rewrote the template-profiles selection + reconciler sections and Files list to the routing + add-only-ensure model, and updated the architecture docs (capture-and-timing.md, the extension install.md) per the user request.

T018.did: Confirmed docs/sidebar.md never documented the right-click Template Profile menu, so no removal was needed.
concerns: No-op task: sidebar.md had no Template-Profile menu reference to remove.

T019.did: Rewrote the unreleased Template-profiles changelog entries in both changelogs to the non-destructive routing model (effect-first, no internal symbol names).

T020.did: Ran compile + the full suite (74 suites / 870 tests pass) and mapped quickstart steps 1–6 to the automated tests covering SC-001..SC-005 and the in-flight/recovery edge cases.
concerns: The manual GUI quickstart walk (installing the extension and clicking through the viewer) was not performed in this headless environment; the behaviors are covered by the unit tests instead.
